---
title: "Evaluation"
pagetitle: "Skein Lite: Evaluation"
execute:
  freeze: true
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
S = json.load(open("../evaluation/reports/site_stats.json"))
R = S["routing"]
AB = json.load(open("../evaluation/reports/gpt_oss_swap_ab.json"))
F = AB["overall"]["faithfulness"]
def pct(x): return f"{x:g}%"
```

::: {.eyebrow}
Every quality claim is a number from a command
:::

Sara answers real shopper questions: "where's my order", "does the Flow Legging run small", "a gift for my mum who loves yoga around \$60". Each fails differently, so quality is measured in layers.

Behind every claim on this page: `{python} S["tests"]` tests, a `{python} S["golden_items"]`-item graded golden set, and a `{python} S["routing_eval_set"]`-turn routing eval. No number here is hand-typed; the page reads them from the committed reports.

## The layers

| Layer | The question it answers | Runs |
|---|---|---|
| **CI eval gate** | Is the pipeline wired: retrieve the fact, or abstain? | every push, offline, no keys |
| **Graded golden set** | Is the answer right, grounded, and complete? | on demand, real providers |
| **Routing eval** | Did the turn reach the right lane? | seconds, at most one 8B call per ambiguous turn |
| **Hallucination defense** | Did the model make something up? | every live turn |

Drift monitoring and the human-gated release loop are operations, so they live on the [MLOps](mlops.qmd) page.

## The graded golden set

`{python} S["golden_items"]` questions in `domains/apparel_ecommerce/eval/golden.jsonl`, English and French. Each row is graded: the facts the answer must contain, the entities behind them, the route, the difficulty, and whether the correct move is to answer or to abstain. A run produces a scorecard, not an impression.

It does three jobs:

1. `make eval` scores retrieval and the abstain gate, overall and per language, with no LLM in the loop.
2. `make ragas` runs the four RAGAS metrics over it with a temperature-0 judge, sanitized against prompt injection.
3. It gates model and cost flips. The worked example is below.

::: {.grid-cards}
::: {.card}
### Faithfulness
Of the claims in the answer, how many does the retrieved context support? The anti-hallucination metric.
:::
::: {.card}
### Answer relevance
Does the answer address the question, or wander?
:::
::: {.card}
### Context precision
Of the chunks retrieved, how many were relevant? Measures the reranker.
:::
::: {.card}
### Context recall
Of the facts needed, how many were retrieved at all?
:::
:::

### The gate in action: a cheaper model, held

Swapping the workhorse to `openai/gpt-oss-120b` looked free: the aggregate RAGAS delta was `{python} AB["aggregate"]["delta"]` over `{python} AB["count"]` graded turns, with the judge fixed to the llama family for both runs. The metric that matters said no:

| Metric | llama-3.3-70b (kept) | gpt-oss-120b (held) |
|---|---:|---:|
| Faithfulness | `{python} F["baseline"]` | `{python} F["candidate"]` |
| Aggregate | `{python} AB["aggregate"]["baseline"]` | `{python} AB["aggregate"]["candidate"]` |

A swap that saves money but invents more facts is not a swap. The full report is `evaluation/reports/gpt_oss_swap_ab.json`; the reasoning is on the [Decisions](decisions.qmd) page.

## The routing scorecard

Every turn goes through a cheap-first cascade (`rag/router.py`): a deterministic escalation intercept, deterministic intent guards, then an 8B tie-break only when neither decides. Send "my jacket arrived torn" to the stylist lane and the best answer in the world is wrong, so routing gets its own labeled eval: `{python} S["routing_eval_set"]` shopper turns, scored with no retrieval and no generation by `scripts/run_agent_eval.py --with-llm`.

| Mode | Accuracy | Escalation recall | Marginal cost |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Deterministic only | `{python} pct(R["deterministic_pct"])` | `{python} pct(R["deterministic_escalation_recall_pct"])` | none |
| **8B tie-break (ships)** | **`{python} pct(R["tiebreak_8b_pct"])`** | **`{python} pct(R["tiebreak_8b_escalation_recall_pct"])`** | ~$0.0005 |
| 70B tie-break (declined) | `{python} pct(R["tiebreak_70b_pct"])` | 100% | ~$0.0006 |

Three findings, each a decision:

- The free deterministic layers carry most turns at zero cost with zero false escalations (precision 100%).
- The 8B tie-break earns its half-cent: it lifts accuracy and takes "get me a person" recall to `{python} pct(R["tiebreak_8b_escalation_recall_pct"])`, so a shopper asking for a human always reaches one.
- The 70B tie-break loses to the 8B (`{python} pct(R["tiebreak_70b_pct"])` vs `{python} pct(R["tiebreak_8b_pct"])`). That is the measured case for staying Groq-only instead of paying a bigger model to classify.

### The eval as a target

The routing eval found the tie-break guessing a lane on ambiguous turns instead of deferring. An [OPRO](https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.03409)-style loop (`mlops/prompt_opt.py`, about eighty lines) rewrote that prompt from its own failures and scored candidates on a held-out split: 73.9% to 79.5%. A human promoted the winner; the loop never edits served code. Why not DSPy is on the [Decisions](decisions.qmd) page.

## Hallucination defense, three layers

The lexical confidence gate abstains on thin evidence before generation. After generation, three layers catch what a prompt cannot guarantee:

| Layer | What it does | When |
|---|---|---|
| **Per-citation grounding check** | A sentence counts as grounded only if it cites a chunk and its content overlaps the specific chunk it cites. Citing [1] while asserting something not in chunk 1 does not count. | every answer, free |
| **Online faithfulness ladder** | Every low-grounding turn plus a deterministic 8% sample is queued after the answer streams. The repo's RAGAS faithfulness judge scores each claim offline, using `gpt-oss-120b`, a different family from the llama generator, so it cannot grade its own style up. It never blocks streaming. | sampled, live traffic |
| **Abstain or escalate** | Thin evidence gets an honest "I don't have that" with a next step. A stuck or upset shopper gets Tiffany, a human, through the review queue. Silence beats invention. | every turn |

`make faithfulness` drains the ladder's queue; a low score lands in the review queue for a person, and feeds the drift signal on the [MLOps](mlops.qmd) page.

## The offline CI gate

Five fixtures over a neutral corpus run on deterministic fakes, no API keys: four must retrieve the right fact, one must abstain. A lexical judge scores them on every push.

[score 1.0]{.chip .good} &nbsp; min 1.0 &nbsp; any single failure blocks the merge

The evaluation notebook breaks the corpus on purpose and watches the score fall. A gate you have never seen fail is a gate you cannot trust.

## Reproduce every number

| Number | Command | Report |
|---|---|---|
| Retrieval and abstain scorecard | `make eval` | `evaluation/reports/` |
| RAGAS answer quality | `make ragas` | logged to MLflow (`skein-ragas`) |
| Routing scorecard | `uv run python scripts/run_agent_eval.py --with-llm` | `evaluation/reports/routing_eval.json` |
| Online faithfulness | `make faithfulness` | `skein-faithfulness` in MLflow |
| This page's headline stats | `make site-stats` | `evaluation/reports/site_stats.json` |
